creator: panden@gmail.com
text:
I was looking for FreeBSD 12.2 Release Checksum Signatures.

* I went to https://www.freebsd.org/releases/12.2R/signatures/ only to find out that the "SHA*"-links take me to https://www.freebsd.org/releases/12.2R/.

This again, presents me to a link to FreeBSD"12.2"-RELEASE signed checksum files, linking back to https://www.freebsd.org/releases/12.2R/signatures/.

I'm sent in a loop.

* This is the case for 12.1 as well (https://www.freebsd.org/releases/12.1R/signatures/)

* For 11.4 (https://www.freebsd.org/releases/11.4R/signatures/) the "SHA*" links to https://www.freebsd.org/releases/11.4R/signatures/, which doesnt get you anywhere.

There is some problems with the links for SD Card Image Checksums as well; it's apparent as soon you go to https://www.freebsd.org/releases/11.4R/signatures/
